hero.headline: MUJ Online MBA in IT and FinTech: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved Super/Dual specialization. 12 spec subjects in Sem 3-4. NAAC A+, NIRF #58.

sections.tldr: MUJ Online MBA in IT and FinTech covers IT infrastructure management, software project management, blockchain for business, fintech foundation, cybersecurity and laws, digital payments, and AI/ML in finance for technology and finance professionals targeting product management or FinTech leadership roles. NAAC A+, NIRF #58 at Rs 1,53,000 upfront (15% off) or Rs 1,80,000. Super/Dual spec — pair with Analytics or Finance. Coursera Plus at Rs 3,999 (70% off).

about.body:
IT and FinTech is the MBA specialization for professionals at the intersection of technology and financial services: software engineers in BFSI, digital payments product managers, fintech analysts, and IT professionals in banking who want to move into Product Head, CTO (FinTech), or digital banking leadership roles. The MUJ Online MBA in IT and FinTech covers the technology-finance interface: IT infrastructure management, software project management, blockchain for business, fintech foundations, cybersecurity and regulatory compliance, digital payments architecture, and AI and ML applications in finance.

MUJ's Super/Dual specialization creates strong credential combinations for FinTech professionals. Pairing IT and FinTech with Analytics and Data Science creates a data-driven financial technology profile for roles at payment gateways and lending platforms. Pairing with Finance creates a complete financial technology and treasury credential for BFSI digital transformation roles. Both combinations are in demand at fintech startups, BFSI digital divisions, and payments companies.

The Coursera Plus bundle at Rs 3,999 gives IT and FinTech students access to blockchain technology, cloud computing, and financial technology courses from Duke University, Princeton, and University of California Irvine — certifications that complement the MUJ curriculum at a fraction of direct enrollment cost.

Manipal University Jaipur Online is NAAC A+ accredited with NIRF #58 University ranking for 2025. EdifyEdu verifies accreditation data independently and does not receive commission from MUJ. Full programme overview at /universities/manipal-university-jaipur-online/mba.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Technology strategy for financial services, digital transformation planning, and aligning IT investment with FinTech business objectives.

sem3Subjects.name: Term Paper
sem3Subjects.description: Independent research paper applying IT or FinTech theory to a real-world financial technology challenge.

sem3Subjects.name: IT Infrastructure Management
sem3Subjects.description: Cloud infrastructure, data centre operations, network management, and IT service continuity for financial services environments.

sem3Subjects.name: Software Project Management
sem3Subjects.description: Agile and waterfall delivery for FinTech products, sprint planning, vendor governance, and regulatory compliance in software delivery.

sem3Subjects.name: Blockchain for Business
sem3Subjects.description: Distributed ledger architecture, smart contracts, DeFi applications, and use cases in banking, payments, and trade finance.

sem3Subjects.name: Fintech Foundation
sem3Subjects.description: FinTech ecosystem overview, regulatory framework (RBI sandbox, PPI guidelines), lending tech, wealthtech, and insurtech models.

sem4Subjects.name: International Business Management
sem4Subjects.description: Cross-border digital payments, global FinTech regulatory environments, and managing international technology operations.

sem4Subjects.name: Project
sem4Subjects.description: Major dissertation applying IT and FinTech specialization to an original applied financial technology or digital banking study.

sem4Subjects.name: Cybersecurity and Laws
sem4Subjects.description: Cybersecurity frameworks for BFSI, IT Act 2000, CERT-In regulations, data protection compliance, and incident response management.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital Payments
sem4Subjects.description: UPI architecture, payment gateway integration, card network operations, BBPS, NACH, and RBI payment system regulations.

sem4Subjects.name: AI and ML in Finance
sem4Subjects.description: Machine learning for credit scoring, fraud detection, algorithmic trading, robo-advisory, and AI-driven risk management.

sem4Subjects.name: Tech-driven Financial Innovation
sem4Subjects.description: Open banking frameworks, API economy in financial services, embedded finance, and product innovation in BFSI technology.

syllabus.note: Semester 1 and 2 subjects are shared across all MUJ Online MBA specializations. Full programme structure at /universities/manipal-university-jaipur-online/mba.

whoHires.body:
IT and FinTech MBA graduates from Manipal University Jaipur Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** FinTech startups, BFSI digital divisions, Payment companies, IT services BFSI vertical, Digital banking platforms

**Entry-level roles:** FinTech Business Analyst, Product Analyst (Payments), IT Project Coordinator (BFSI), Cybersecurity Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Product Manager (FinTech), IT Manager (BFSI), Digital Payments Manager, Blockchain Project Lead, Cybersecurity Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Product (FinTech), CTO (FinTech startup), Head of Digital Banking, Chief Information Security Officer, Director of Technology (BFSI)

**Top hiring organisations:** Razorpay, PhonePe, Paytm, BillDesk, HDFC Bank Digital, ICICI Bank Technology, Axis Bank FinTech, TCS BFSI, Infosys FinTech, Wipro BFSI

skills.body:
The IT and FinTech specialization builds skills that BFSI technology and FinTech employers test at product manager and technology director levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Digital Payments: UPI integration architecture, payment gateway APIs, PCI-DSS compliance, and NPCI product frameworks
- Cybersecurity: ISO 27001 for BFSI, RBI cybersecurity framework, threat modelling, and IT audit for financial systems
- Blockchain: Ethereum and Hyperledger Fabric basics, smart contract deployment, and blockchain use case assessment for BFSI
- AI/ML in Finance: logistic regression for credit risk, XGBoost for fraud detection, and NLP for financial document processing

**Applied skills:**
- FinTech Product Management: product roadmap prioritisation for payment and lending products, regulatory sprint planning, and go-to-market for FinTech features
- Software Delivery Governance: release management for regulated environments, change management controls, and software audit trails

**Soft skills:** Presenting technology investment proposals to BFSI boards, managing regulatory relationships with RBI and SEBI for technology compliance, and translating fintech product strategy for non-technical finance leadership.

comparisons.body:
Comparing MUJ Online MBA IT and FinTech against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA IT Management:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity IT Management covers IT governance, data privacy, and agile delivery. MUJ IT and FinTech is specifically oriented to financial technology — blockchain, digital payments, AI in finance, and FinTech regulation. For professionals in BFSI technology, MUJ's spec is more targeted. For general IT management careers, Amity is competitive.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA IT Management:** NMIMS is NAAC A++ and NIRF Management #24 at Rs 1,96,000-2,20,000. NMIMS IT Management covers cybersecurity and AI applications. MUJ IT and FinTech adds digital payments architecture, blockchain, and FinTech-specific regulatory content. For careers specifically in payment companies and BFSI digital, MUJ is more focused. NMIMS brand is stronger for IT management roles at large corporates.

**vs LPU Online MBA IT Management:** LPU is NAAC A++ at competitive fees. LPU's IT spec covers software and systems management. MUJ's FinTech overlay (digital payments, blockchain, AI in finance) is a differentiated profile for fintech and BFSI technology hiring. Manipal brand is well recognised at large BFSI technology employers and IT services firms with BFSI verticals.

fees.body:
The total program fee for the Manipal University Jaipur Online MBA in IT and FinTech can be paid through 2 options:

**Option 1 — Semester-wise payment**: Rs 45,000 per semester, Rs 1,80,000 total across 4 semesters
**Option 2 — Full upfront payment (discounted)**: Rs 1,53,000 one-time (Rs 1,80,000 standard less 15 percent fee concession)

A one-time registration fee of Rs 500 applies at enrolment. MUJ does not charge separate exam fees per attempt, project evaluation fees, or admission processing fees beyond this.

For working professionals, MUJ offers a 24-month zero-cost EMI plan at approximately Rs 7,500 per month on the standard fee (Rs 6,375 per month on the discounted fee) through its NBFC lending partners.

**Scholarships available** (non-stackable — pick one based on eligibility):
- **Divyaang** (persons with disabilities): 20% discount with Divyaang certificate
- **Defence personnel**: 20% discount with Service/IC number
- **Manipal University Jaipur alumni** (UG or PG): 20% discount
- **Government employees**: 10% discount with Govt ID
- **Merit scholarship**: 10% discount for 80%+ marks in 10+2 or bachelor's

**Coursera Plus bundled access** (MUJ differentiator): Enrolled MUJ students get Coursera Plus at Rs 3,999 (standard Rs 13,999 — a 70 percent discount). This includes 10,000+ courses from University of Virginia, University of Michigan, Johns Hopkins, University of California Irvine, and other top global universities.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees and scholarships with Manipal University Jaipur each quarter. Please reconfirm current offers and eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The Manipal University Jaipur degree certificate carries the Manipal University Jaipur name, the MBA designation, and identifies the online learning mode per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The IT and FinTech specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode identification on the certificate is standard for UGC-DEB approved programmes and equivalent in recognition to regular MBA degrees for employment, promotions, and further studies.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from MUJ Online MBA IT and FinTech alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Karan Mehta
items.liked: Digital payments and AI/ML in finance subjects were immediately applicable — I used the UPI architecture framework in a product spec the week after studying it
items.disliked: Blockchain content was theoretical and did not cover implementation depth needed for actual smart contract roles
items.body: I work as a product manager at a payments company. The digital payments, cybersecurity, and FinTech foundation subjects were the most applied MBA content I have encountered. Pairing IT and FinTech with Analytics gave me a data-driven product credential. The Coursera Plus access let me complete a blockchain specialization from Princeton at Rs 3,999 total. Manipal brand was recognised without question at Razorpay and PhonePe during my job switch.

items.name: Deepa Krishnan
items.liked: Cybersecurity and laws subject covered RBI cybersecurity framework in depth — directly relevant to my IT audit work at an NBFC
items.disliked: Live session scheduling was inflexible for IST evening timing when professors were calling in from different time zones
items.body: I handle IT compliance at an NBFC. The cybersecurity, IT Act, and CERT-In content was substantive and current. Pairing IT and FinTech with Finance helped me understand the treasury and regulatory capital side of the digital banking projects I audit. The Manipal degree was recognised by my employer's HR for promotion eligibility without any issues. The Coursera Plus access was good value for the IBM cybersecurity courses.

items.name: Rohit Sinha
items.liked: Tech-driven financial innovation subject introduced open banking and embedded finance frameworks I was not familiar with
items.disliked: Career services had no connections with FinTech companies or BFSI digital divisions. The job board had generic postings, nothing fintech-specific
items.body: The content covered the right topics for a FinTech-focused MBA. AI in finance, digital payments, and the fintech regulatory landscape were practical. My issue was placement — the online career cell has no relationships with fintech hiring teams. All four of my final interview calls came through LinkedIn and direct referrals. Join this programme for the credential and the Coursera Plus access, not for placement support in the fintech sector.

faqs.question: Can I choose dual specialization with the MUJ Online MBA in IT and FinTech?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Super/Dual model lets you pair IT and FinTech with a second specialization at no extra cost. Strong combinations: IT and FinTech plus Analytics and Data Science (for data-driven product and BI roles in payments and lending), IT and FinTech plus Finance (for digital banking and treasury technology roles), or IT and FinTech plus BFSI (for comprehensive financial services technology coverage). Both specs appear on your marksheet.

faqs.question: Is Coursera Plus access included or charged separately?
faqs.answer: Coursera Plus is available to enrolled MUJ students at Rs 3,999 per year (standard Rs 13,999 — 70% off). For IT and FinTech students, valuable Coursera offerings include blockchain specializations from Princeton University, financial technology courses from the University of Michigan, and cloud computing certifications from Google and IBM. At Rs 3,999, these complement the MUJ curriculum at near-zero cost.

faqs.question: What scholarships are available for the MUJ Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Five scholarship categories: 20% for Divyaang individuals, 20% for Defence personnel, 20% for MUJ alumni, 10% for Government employees, and 10% merit scholarship for 80%+ marks. Non-stackable. Plus: full upfront payment gets a 15% fee concession — Rs 1,80,000 becomes Rs 1,53,000.

faqs.question: What salary can IT and FinTech MBA graduates from MUJ expect?
faqs.answer: FinTech product managers and IT managers in BFSI with 4-7 years of experience and this MBA earn Rs 14-28 lakh at payment companies and digital banking teams. VP Product and Head of Digital Banking roles pay Rs 25-50 lakh. CTO roles at mid-size FinTech startups pay Rs 30-60 lakh. Outcomes depend on domain specialization (payments vs lending vs wealth), company scale, and depth of technical expertise alongside the MBA.

faqs.question: Is this MBA suitable for non-technical professionals in FinTech?
faqs.answer: Yes. The IT and FinTech spec is designed for the management-technology interface, not deep programming. Business analysts, product managers, compliance professionals, and operations executives in FinTech can complete this programme without a software engineering background. The MBA foundation (Sem 1-2) provides the business context; the spec subjects provide the FinTech domain knowledge. Technical roles requiring coding skills need additional engineering credentials.

chooseIf: You work in payments, digital banking, BFSI technology, or FinTech and want a NAAC A+ MBA covering blockchain, digital payments, cybersecurity, and AI in finance under Rs 1.8 lakh.
You want to pair IT and FinTech with Analytics or Finance for a data-driven or financial technology management credential using MUJ's Super/Dual spec.
You value Coursera Plus at Rs 3,999 for blockchain, financial technology, and cloud certifications from Princeton, Michigan, and other top universities.
consider: You need the strongest brand for FinTech product leadership at a Tier 1 institution — ISB's fintech programmes carry stronger employer recognition for BFSI technology director roles.
You are a deep technical professional in cybersecurity or blockchain — CISSP, CEH, or Hyperledger certifications are more role-specific than an MBA for specialist technology positions.
You need active FinTech sector placement support — MUJ online career services do not maintain connections with fintech hiring teams or BFSI digital divisions.
